doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, um, I’m here because I’ve had this dry cough for about two weeks now. It was worse last week, but it’s kinda getting better. I also have a clear runny nose that started about a week ago.

doctor_turn: Can you tell me where the cough feels the worst?
patient_turn: Honestly, it doesn’t really feel like it’s, um, localized anywhere specific. It’s just a dry cough, you know? I don’t have any chest pain or anything like that, so it’s not super painful or anything.

doctor_turn: How has the cough changed since it first started?
patient_turn: Well, um, it was definitely worse last week, like really persistent. But now it’s, uh, getting better, I think. It’s not as frequent, which is a relief, honestly.

doctor_turn: Where exactly do you feel the cough in your chest or throat?
patient_turn: Uh, I mostly feel it in my throat, I guess? It’s not really in my chest, more like a tickle that makes me cough. It’s just kinda annoying, you know?

doctor_turn: What does the cough feel like? Is it dry, or do you feel any tightness?
patient_turn: Oh, it's definitely dry, but sometimes it feels kind of tight too. I mean, I don't really have any tightness at all, actually.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the cough starting?
patient_turn: Um, I think I first noticed it about two weeks ago. It just kinda came out of nowhere, you know?

doctor_turn: When did the cough first start?
Do you have any ongoing medical conditions or health issues?
patient_turn: No, um, I don’t have any ongoing medical conditions or health issues. I’ve been pretty healthy overall, to be honest.

doctor_turn: Are you taking any medications right now?
patient_turn: Uh, no, I’m not taking any medications right now. I haven’t needed any, so… yeah, nothing at all.

doctor_turn: Do you have any known allergies to medications, foods, or anything else?
patient_turn: Um, no, I don’t have any known allergies to medications or foods or anything like that. I’m pretty sure I’m all good in that department.

doctor_turn: Does anyone in your family have a history of high cholesterol?
patient_turn: Yeah, um, my dad has a history of high cholesterol. That’s pretty much the only thing in my family that I know of, though.

doctor_turn: Have you traveled outside the province in the last few weeks?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t traveled outside the province at all in the last few weeks. I’ve just been, um, at home doing virtual university stuff.

doctor_turn: Do you smoke or use any recreational drugs regularly?
patient_turn: Uh, I don’t smoke regularly, but I’ve, like, experimented with smoking and marijuana occasionally. But it’s not something I do all the time, you know?

doctor_turn: How often do you drink alcohol, and how much do you usually have?
patient_turn: I drink alcohol socially on weekends during school terms, um, but I don’t really keep track of how much. It’s usually just a few drinks with friends, you know? Nothing crazy.

doctor_turn: Did your mother have any respiratory symptoms recently?
patient_turn: Yeah, um, my mom had some mild upper respiratory symptoms last week. She didn’t get tested for COVID-19 or anything, though. It was just, you know, a bit of a runny nose and stuff.

doctor_turn: Have you been tested for COVID-19 since your symptoms started?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t been tested for COVID-19 yet. I was advised to self-isolate pending test results, so, um, I’m just waiting on that.
